name: Peanut Butter Fudge
recipeInstructions:
1. Line an 8 x 8 x 2 inches baking pan with foil, extending foil over the edges of the pan.
2. Butter the foil and set the pan aside.
3. Melt butter in a heavy 2-quart saucepan.
4. In the saucepan, combine sugar, milk, peanut butter, and corn syrup. Cook over medium-high heat to boiling, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon to dissolve sugar. This should take about 5 minutes. Avoid splashing the mixture on the sides of the pan.
5. Carefully clip a candy thermometer into the side of the pan.
6. Continue cooking over medium-low heat, stirring frequently, until the thermometer registers 234°F (soft-ball stage). The mixture should boil at a moderate, steady rate over the entire surface. Reaching the soft-ball stage should take 8 to 10 minutes.
7. Remove the pan from the heat and add 2 tablespoons margarine and vanilla. Do not stir.
8. Allow the mixture to cool to 110°F for about 55 minutes.
9. Remove the thermometer and beat vigorously until just beginning to thicken; add nuts.
10. Continue beating until very thick but still glossy. This should take an additional 6 to 7 minutes.
11. Quickly turn the fudge into the prepared pan. The candy will lose its gloss once it is turned in the pan.
12. While the fudge is warm, score it into 1-inch squares.
13. When the candy is firm, lift it out of the pan and cut into squares. Store tightly covered.
